title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## 2013 Census QuickStats about Māori
Information about Māori people living in New Zealand, and how life has changed for this ethnic group since 2006.

## 2013 Census QuickStats about national highlights
Information about demographic, social, and economic subjects at a national level.

## New Zealand as a village of 100 people
This infographic presents an overview of New Zealand's population in 2013.

## Privacy impact assessment of integrating Inland Revenue-sources tax data in the IDI with the Survey of Family, Income, and Employment
Outlines the purpose and benefits of the April 2013 proposal to link Inland Revenue tax data in the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) with data from the Survey of Family, Income, and Employment (SoFIE) from Statistics NZ.

## QuickStats about a place posters
Posters about communities who live in New Zealand's cities, districts, and Auckland local board areas. The information is also available in the 2013 Census QuickStats about a place reports.

## Differences between the full HES and HES (Income)
Outlines differences between the Household Economic Survey (HES), which runs every three years, and the Household Economic Survey (Income), which runs in each of the two years between the full HES.

## Time use survey 2009/10 – supplementary tables
Download time use supplementary tables that are extensions of the Time use survey: 2009/10 information release tables.

## New Zealand income survey super SURF
Download the New Zealand Income Survey (NZIS) Super SURF dataset, based on data from the NZIS: June 2003 quarter.

## LEED quarterly technical notes
Technical notes about the quarterly statistics produced from the Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED).

## Seasonal employment patterns in the horticultural industry
This study uses Linked-Employer-Employee Data (LEED) to examine short-term and seasonal employment patterns within the New Zealand horticulture industry.

## Linked Employer-Employee Data Project: Project Scope
This Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED) project looks at business and employment performance and dynamics by utilising existing data sources.
